parameter.label: Recipient List
parameter.tooltip: Comma or semicolon list of recipients (usernames, SMTP addresses, team names, Teams channels, or Slack channels). Teams channels use format ‘Teams:Webhook URL’. Slack channels use format ‘Slack:Channel Name’. Wrap values in double quotes if they contain separator characters.

parameter.label: Notification Template Name
parameter.tooltip: Name of the notification template to use when sending this email

parameter.label: JSON Variables
parameter.tooltip: JSON input of miscellaneous values. These values are used to replace placeholder content in notification messages.

parameter.label: Kapp Slug Initiating Notification
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the Kapp sending the notification (ex: services, queue, etc.)

parameter.label: Related Submission Id
parameter.tooltip: Id of the submission to get values for, required if using those or attachments in the message

parameter.label: Form Slug Initiating Notification
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the Form sending the notification (ex: work-order, cleaning, etc.)

parameter.label: Data Source:
parameter.tooltip: The source that contains the data to use to create each task in the loop.

parameter.label: Loop Path:
parameter.tooltip: The XPath statement to indicate what data records to process.

parameter.label: Variable Name:
parameter.tooltip: The local variable name used to represent the data used in loop tasks.

parameter.label: Type:
parameter.tooltip: How many loop processes must be completed before continuing?

parameter.label: Number:
parameter.tooltip: If some, how many?

task.label: Has Teams or Slack Notifications
task.value: emails = JSON.parse(@results['Collect Results']['output']).select{|item| item['method'] == "teams" || item['method'] == "slack"} emails.size > 0

parameter.label: Data Source:
parameter.tooltip: The source that contains the data to use to create each task in the loop.

parameters.parameter:
<%= #supports comma or semi-colon separated lists. Items can be wrapped in double quotes to support values that may include one of the separators
@inputs['Recipient List'].split(/[,;](?=(?:[^"]*"[^"]*")*[^"]*$)/).map(&:strip)
%>

parameter.label: Loop Path:
parameter.tooltip: The XPath statement to indicate what data records to process.

parameter.label: Variable Name:
parameter.tooltip: The local variable name used to represent the data used in loop tasks.

parameter.label: Type:
parameter.tooltip: How many loop processes must be completed before continuing?

parameter.label: Number:
parameter.tooltip: If some, how many?

parameter.label: Connection ID
parameter.tooltip: Connection ID

parameter.label: Operation ID
parameter.tooltip: Operation ID

parameters.parameter: <%= value = @results['Sanitize Recipients - Start']['Value'] if value.start_with?('"') && value.end_with?('"') value[1..-2] # Remove both quotes elsif value.start_with?('"') value[1..-1] # Remove just the leading quote else value # No quotes, leave as-is end %>

parameter.label: Connection ID
parameter.tooltip: Connection ID

parameter.label: Operation ID
parameter.tooltip: Operation ID

parameters.parameter: <%= value = @results['Sanitize Recipients - Start']['Value'] if value.start_with?('"') && value.end_with?('"') value[1..-2] # Remove both quotes elsif value.start_with?('"') value[1..-1] # Remove just the leading quote else value # No quotes, leave as-is end %>

task.name: Is Valid Email, Teams or Slack format

parameters.parameter:
<%= # !(/^"?(teams:|slack:)\s*.+|^"?[A-Z0-9._%+-~'?]+@[A-Z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z]{2,}$/i.match("#{@results['Sanitize Recipients - Start']['Value']}").nil?)
value = @results['Sanitize Recipients - Start']['Value'] if value.start_with?('"') && value.end_with?('"') value = value[1..-2] # Remove both quotes elsif value.start_with?('"') value = value[1..-1] # Remove just the leading quote else value # No quotes, leave as-is end
!(/^(teams:|slack:)\s*.+|^[A-Z0-9._%+-~'?]+@[A-Z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z]{2,}$/i.match(value).nil?) %>

task.label: Valid
task.value: @results['Is Valid Email, Teams or Slack format']['output'] === "true"

task.name: Format Email, Teams, or Slack JSON

parameters.parameter:
<%= value = @results['Sanitize Recipients - Start']['Value'] # Remove optional quotes clean_value = value.strip if clean_value.start_with?('"') && clean_value.end_with?('"') clean_value = clean_value[1..-2] # Remove both first and last character elsif clean_value.start_with?('"') clean_value = clean_value[1..-1] # Remove just the leading quote end value_lower = clean_value.downcase
if value_lower.start_with?("teams:") ndm = "teams" ndt = clean_value.split(":", 2)[1]&.strip || "" elsif value_lower.start_with?("slack:") ndm = "slack" ndt = clean_value.split(":", 2)[1]&.strip || "" else ndm = "email" ndt = clean_value end
{ "type" => "broadcast", "method"=> ndm, "target" => ndt, "language"=> "- Default -", "cid" => JSON.parse(@results['Get Space']['Attributes Map'])['CID'][0] }.to_json %>

<%= targets = [] recipient_details = []
if @results.key? 'Format User JSON' @results['Format User JSON'].collect do |user| parsed_user = JSON.parse(user[1]['output']) unless targets.include?(parsed_user['target']) recipient_details.push(parsed_user) targets.push(parsed_user['target']) end end end
if @results.key? 'Format Team JSON' @results['Format Team JSON'].collect do |users| JSON.parse(users[1]['output']).each do |user| unless targets.include?(user['target']) recipient_details.push(user) targets.push(user['target']) end end end end
if @results.key? 'Format Group Notification Target JSON' @results['Format Group Notification Target JSON'].collect do |target_group| JSON.parse(target_group[1]['output']).each do |target| unless targets.include?(target['target']) recipient_details.push(target) targets.push(target['target']) end end end end
if @results.key? 'Format Email, Teams, or Slack JSON' @results['Format Email, Teams, or Slack JSON'].collect do |target| parsed_target = JSON.parse(target[1]['output']) unless targets.include?(parsed_target['target']) recipient_details.push(parsed_target) targets.push(parsed_target['target']) end end end
recipient_details.select{|user| !user['target'].to_s.include?('@kinops.io')}.to_json %>

parameter.label: Error Handling
parameter.tooltip: Determine what to return if an error is encountered.

parameter.label: Method
parameter.tooltip: GET,POST,PUT,PATCH,DELETE (Defaults to GET)

parameter.label: Path
parameter.tooltip: Example: /kapps/:kappSlug/forms/:formSlug

parameters.parameter:
<%= ### SUBMISSIONS ### related_submission_id = @inputs['Related Submission Id'] has_related_submission = !related_submission_id.to_s.empty? submission_includes = "details,origin,parent,type,values,form,form.attributes,form.kapp,form.kapp.attributes,form.kapp.space,form.kapp.space.attributes"
### FORMS ### related_form_slug = @inputs['Form Slug Initiating Notification'] has_related_form = !related_form_slug.to_s.empty? form_includes = "details,attributes,kapp,kapp.attributes,kapp.space,kapp.space.attributes"
### KAPPS ### related_kapp_slug = @inputs['Kapp Slug Initiating Notification'] has_related_kapp = !related_form_slug.to_s.empty? kapp_includes = "details,attributes,space,space.attributes"
### ROUTE VARIABLE ### data_api_route = "/"
if has_related_submission data_api_route << "submissions/#{related_submission_id}?include=#{submission_includes}" elsif has_related_form data_api_route << "kapps/#{related_kapp_slug}/forms/#{related_form_slug}?include=#{form_includes}" elsif has_related_kapp data_api_route = "/kapps/#{related_kapp_slug}?include=#{kapp_includes}" else data_api_route = '/space?include=attributes,details' end
data_api_route%>

parameter.label: Body
parameter.tooltip: JSON body if applicable (POST,PUT,PATCH)

parameter.label: Error Handling
parameter.tooltip: Determine what to return if an error is encountered.

parameter.label: Space Slug
parameter.tooltip: The id of the space from which the submission is being retrieved.

parameter.label: Recipient Json Object
parameter.tooltip: The recipient object should look like this. {"type":"user","username":"test@mycompany.com","smtpaddress":{"to":"to_test@mycompany.com","cc":"cc_test@mycompany.com","bcc":"bcc_test@mycompany.com"},"language":"- Default -","email notifications":"yes"}

parameters.parameter:
<%= # Just passing in an email address will work as well as the formatted object. #if JSON.parse(@results['Send Message Loop Start']['Value'])['type'] == 'user' # JSON.parse(@results['Fetch User Data']['Response Body'])['user']['email'] #elsif JSON.parse(@results['Send Message Loop Start']['Value'])['type'] == 'smtp' # JSON.parse(@results['Send Message Loop Start']['Value'])['recipient'] #end JSON.parse(@results['Send Message Loop Start']['Value'])['target']
%>

parameter.label: Notification Template Name
parameter.tooltip: The notification template name being used.

parameter.label: Replacement Values
parameter.tooltip: JSON string of replacement values. Required to successfully apply replacements to any notification template. JSON keys can be 'form', 'values', and 'vars'.

parameter.label: Submission Id
parameter.tooltip: Needed if the message contains attachments.

parameters.parameter:
<%= ## Parse Replacement Variables Data data_response = @results['Fetch Substitution Data']['Handler Error Message'].nil? ? JSON.parse(@results['Fetch Substitution Data']['Response Body']) : nil user_response = (@results.has_key?('Fetch User Data') && @results['Fetch User Data']['Handler Error Message'].nil?) ? JSON.parse(@results['Fetch User Data']['Response Body']) : nil addtl_vars = @inputs['JSON Variables'].to_s.empty? ? nil : JSON.parse(@inputs['JSON Variables']) backup_vars = {"spaceAttributes" => {"Theme Color Primary" => "#666666"}}
### SUBMISSIONS ### related_submission_id = @inputs['Related Submission Id'] has_related_submission = !related_submission_id.to_s.empty?
### FORMS ### related_form_slug = @inputs['Form Slug Initiating Notification'] has_related_form = !related_form_slug.to_s.empty?
### KAPPS ### related_kapp_slug = @inputs['Kapp Slug Initiating Notification'] has_related_kapp = !related_form_slug.to_s.empty?
### DATASTORE ### #is_datastore = ['yes', 'true'].include?(@inputs['Is Datastore'].to_s.downcase)
api_context = "" if has_related_submission api_context = "Submission" #elsif has_related_form && (has_related_kapp || is_datastore) elsif has_related_form && has_related_kapp api_context = "Form" elsif has_related_kapp api_context = "Kapp" else api_context = "Space" end
@variables = {} variableTypes = [ "submission", "values", "form", "formAttributes", "kapp", "kappAttributes", "space", "spaceAttributes", "vars", "user", "userAttributes", "userProfileAttributes" ] variableTypes.each do |var| @variables[var] = {} end
def buildSubmissionVariables(data) data = data['submission'] data.keys.each do |key| # Build Up Submission Properties (non hash / arrays) if !data[key].is_a?(Hash) && !data[key].is_a?(Array) @variables['submission'][key] = data[key] end
# Pass Form Object to the Build Form Variables Routine to Handle the Rest buildFormVariables('form' => data[key]) if key == 'form'
# Build Submission Values Variables @variables['values'] = data[key] if key == 'values' end end
def buildFormVariables(data) data = data['form'] data.keys.each do |key| # Build Up Form Properties (non hash / arrays) if !data[key].is_a?(Hash) && !data[key].is_a?(Array) label = 'Form ' + key.capitalize @variables['form'][label] = data[key] end
# Build Attributes for this object if key == 'attributes' data[key].each do |obj| @variables['formAttributes'][obj['name']] = obj['values'].join(', ') end end
# Pass Kapp Object to the Build Kapp Variables Routine to Handle the Rest buildKappVariables('kapp' => data[key]) if key == 'kapp'
# Pass Kapp Object to the Build Space Variables Routine to Handle the Rest # (for Datastore submissions) buildSpaceVariables('space' => data[key]) if key == 'space' end end
def buildKappVariables(data) data = data['kapp'] data.keys.each do |key| # Build Up Kapp Properties (non hash / arrays) if !data[key].is_a?(Hash) && !data[key].is_a?(Array) label = 'Kapp ' + key.capitalize @variables['kapp'][label] = data[key] end
# Build Attributes for this object if key == 'attributes' data[key].each do |obj| @variables['kappAttributes'][obj['name']] = obj['values'].join(', ') end end
# Pass Space Object to the Build Space Variables Routine to Handle the Rest buildSpaceVariables('space' => data[key]) if key == 'space' end end
def buildSpaceVariables(data) data = data['space'] data.keys.each do |key| # Build Up Space Properties (non hash / arrays) if !data[key].is_a?(Hash) && !data[key].is_a?(Array) label = 'Space ' + key.capitalize @variables['space'][label] = data[key] end
# Build Attributes for this object next unless key == 'attributes' data[key].each do |obj| @variables['spaceAttributes'][obj['name']] = obj['values'].join(', ') end end end
def buildUserVariables(data) data = data['user'] data.keys.each do |key| # Build Up User Properties (non hash / arrays) if !data[key].is_a?(Hash) && !data[key].is_a?(Array) @variables['user'][key] = data[key] end
# Build Attributes for this object if key == 'attributes' data[key].each do |obj| @variables['userAttributes'][obj['name']] = obj['values'].join(', ') end end
# Build Profile Attributes for this object next unless key == 'profileAttributes' data[key].each do |obj| @variables['userProfileAttributes'][obj['name']] = obj['values'].join(', ') end end end
if !data_response.nil? # Build variables from data if api_context == 'Submission' buildSubmissionVariables(data_response) elsif api_context == 'Form' buildFormVariables(data_response) elsif api_context == 'Kapp' buildKappVariables(data_response) else buildSpaceVariables(data_response) end end
# Add User Variables if supplied if !user_response.nil? buildUserVariables(user_response) end
# Add Additional Variables @variables['vars'] = addtl_vars unless addtl_vars.nil?
# Check for Backups if supplied unless backup_vars.nil? # Loop over Each Backup Key (kapp / submission / values / user...etc) backup_vars.keys.each do |key| # If the backup is a valid property of what variables are expected next if @variables[key].nil? # Loop over each backup value supplied and check if it exists backup_vars[key].each do |k, v| # If no value was found, use the backup value if @variables[key][k].nil? || @variables[key][k].empty? @variables[key][k] = v end end end end
@variables.to_json%>

parameter.label: Error Handling
parameter.tooltip: Determine what to return if an error is encountered.

parameter.label: Agent Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the agent that contains the handler to execute. (Configured in Space > Platform Components > Agents)

parameter.label: Handler Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the handler to execute. ex on-prem-incident-create

parameter.label: Payload
parameter.tooltip: The parameter-to-value mappings of the handler that is being executed in JSON format. ie {"baz": "buz"}

parameters.parameter:
<%= recipient = "" if JSON.parse(@results['Send Message Loop Start']['Value'])['type'] == 'user' recipient = JSON.parse(@results['Fetch User Data']['Response Body'])['user']['email'] elsif JSON.parse(@results['Send Message Loop Start']['Value'])['type'] == 'smtp' recipient = JSON.parse(@results['Send Message Loop Start']['Value'])['recipient'] end
{'error_handling' => 'Raise Error', 'space_slug' => '', 'recipient_json' => recipient, 'notification_template_name' => @inputs['Notification Template Name'], 'replacement_values' => @results['Replacement Values For Email']['output'], 'submission_id' => @inputs['Related Submission Id'] }.to_json %>

This routine contains the workflow necessary to use this routine in environments that use either local handlers or agent handlers.
Use the instructions below to connect the correct nodes for the enviroment
Local Handlers: -Connect the following node - 'Junction' to 'Assemble and Send Message' - 'Assemble and Send Message' to 'Send Message Loop End' - Remove any connectors to and from 'Assemble and Send Message - Agent'
Agent Handlers: -Connect the following node - 'Junction' to 'Assemble and Send Message - Agent' - 'Assemble and Send Message - Agent' to 'Send Message Loop End' - Remove any connectors to and from 'Assemble and Send Message'

<%= #email_notifications = 'yes'
language = '- Default -' if !@results['Fetch User']['Preferred Locale'].to_s.empty? language = @results['Fetch User']['Preferred Locale'] end
target = nil profile_attrs = JSON.parse(@results['Fetch User']['Profile Attributes']) if profile_attrs.key?('Notification Delivery Target') && profile_attrs['Notification Delivery Target'][0] && !profile_attrs['Notification Delivery Target'][0].to_s.strip.empty? target = JSON.parse(@results['Fetch User']['Profile Attributes'])['Notification Delivery Target'][0] else unless /^[A-Z0-9._%+-~'?]+@[A-Z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z]{2,}$/i.match((@results['Fetch User']['Email']).to_s).nil? target = @results['Fetch User']['Email'] end end
format = {} #format['type'] = 'smtp' format['type'] = 'individual' format['method'] = @results['User Delivery Method']['output'] format['username'] = @results['Fetch User']['Username'] #format['smtpaddress'] = email format['target'] = target format['language'] = language #format['email notifications'] = email_notifications #format['delivery_method'] = @results['User Delivery Method']['output'] format['cid'] = JSON.parse(@results['Fetch User']['Attributes'])['CID'][0] || JSON.parse(@results['Get Space']['Attributes Map'])['CID'][0]
format.to_json%>

<%= delivery_method = 'email'
ndm = JSON.parse(@results['Fetch User']['Profile Attributes'])['Notification Delivery Method'][0] if !ndm.to_s.strip.empty? if ['email','teams','slack','none'].include?(ndm.strip.downcase) delivery_method = ndm.strip.downcase end end
delivery_method %>

<%= ndt = JSON.parse(@results['Fetch Team']['Notification Delivery Target'])
delivery_method = 'individual' if !@results['Fetch Team']['Notification Delivery Method'].to_s.strip.empty? ndm = @results['Fetch Team']['Notification Delivery Method'].strip.downcase if ['group email','teams','slack','none'].include?(ndm) && ndt.size > 0 delivery_method = @results['Fetch Team']['Notification Delivery Method'].strip.downcase end end
delivery_method %>

<%= formatted_users = [] ##### Fetch Team w/memberships now includes all details. # Parse Team Members #team_members = JSON.parse(@results['Fetch Team']['Memberships JSON']).collect { |member| member['user']['username'] } # Parse Users and only filter members #team_users = JSON.parse(@results['Retrieve Users']['Users List JSON']).select { |user| team_members.include? user['username'] } # Format Each Team User #team_users.each do |user|
JSON.parse(@results['Fetch Team']['Memberships']).each do |user| username = user['User Name'] # This is here in preparation for when someone can turn off notifications #email_notifications = 'yes' ndm = (user.dig('Profile Attributes Map', 'Notification Delivery Method')&.first || "email").downcase
language = '- Default -' unless user['User Preferred Locale'].to_s.empty? language = user['User Preferred Locale'] end
email = nil unless /^[A-Z0-9._%+-~'?]+@[A-Z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z]{2,}$/i.match((user['User Email']).to_s).nil? email = user['User Email'] end
format = {} #format['type'] = 'user' format['type'] = 'individual' format['methode'] = ndm #format['username'] = user['username'] format['username'] = username #format['smtpaddress'] = email format['target'] = email format['language'] = language #format['email notifications'] = email_notifications formatted_users << format end
formatted_users.to_json%>

<%= cid = JSON.parse(@results['Fetch Team']['CID'])[0] || JSON.parse(@results['Get Space']['Attributes Map'])['CID'][0]
ndm = @results['Team Delivery Method']['output'] if ndm == "group email" ndm = "email" end
ndt = JSON.parse(@results['Fetch Team']['Notification Delivery Target']) group_targets=[]
ndt.each{|target| group_targets.push({ #"type"=> "smtp", "type" => "broadcast", "method"=> ndm, "target" => target.strip, "language"=> "- Default -", "cid" => cid }) }
group_targets.to_json %>

task.name: Send Message - Teams or Slack - Loop Start

parameter.label: Data Source:
parameter.tooltip: The source that contains the data to use to create each task in the loop.

parameters.parameter: <%= emails = JSON.parse(@results['Collect Results']['output']).select{|item| item['method'] == "teams" || item['method'] == "slack"} emails.to_json %>

parameter.label: Loop Path:
parameter.tooltip: The XPath or JSONPath statement to indicate what data records to process.

parameter.label: Variable Name:
parameter.tooltip: The local variable name used to represent the data used in loop tasks.

task.name: Send Message - Teams or Slack - Loop End

parameter.label: Type:
parameter.tooltip: How many loop processes must be completed before continuing?

parameter.label: Number:
parameter.tooltip: If some, how many?

parameter.label: Connection ID
parameter.tooltip: Connection ID

parameter.label: Operation ID
parameter.tooltip: Operation ID

parameter.label: Connection ID
parameter.tooltip: Connection ID

parameter.label: Operation ID
parameter.tooltip: Operation ID

parameters.parameter: <%= JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Teams or Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['cid'] %>

task.label: Teams + Found Client
task.value: JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Teams or Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['method'] == "teams" && @results['Get Client Configuration']['_Returned Count'].to_i > 0 && !JSON.parse(@results['Get Client Configuration']['submissions'])[0]['agent handler'].to_s.empty?

task.label: Slack + Found Client
task.value: JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Teams or Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['method'] == "slack" && @results['Get Client Configuration']['_Returned Count'].to_i > 0 && !JSON.parse(@results['Get Client Configuration']['submissions'])[0]['agent handler slack'].to_s.empty?

parameter.label: Connection ID
parameter.tooltip: Connection ID

parameter.label: Operation ID
parameter.tooltip: Operation ID

parameter.label: Connection ID
parameter.tooltip: Connection ID

parameter.label: Operation ID
parameter.tooltip: Operation ID

parameter.label: Connection ID
parameter.tooltip: Connection ID

parameter.label: Operation ID
parameter.tooltip: Operation ID

task.name: Get Slack Id by Email - Agent

parameter.label: Error Handling
parameter.tooltip: Determine what to return if an error is encountered.

parameter.label: Agent Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the agent that contains the handler to execute. (Configured in Space > Platform Components > Agents)

parameter.label: Handler Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the handler to execute. ex on-prem-incident-create

parameter.label: Payload
parameter.tooltip: The parameter-to-value mappings of the handler that is being executed in JSON format. ie {"baz": "buz"}

parameters.parameter: <%= target = JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['target'] encoded_target = URI.encode_www_form_component(target) { "error_handling" => "Raise Error", "method" => "GET", "path" => "/users.lookupByEmail?email=#{encoded_target}", }.to_json %>

parameter.label: Error Handling
parameter.tooltip: Determine what to return if an error is encountered.

parameter.label: Agent Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the agent that contains the handler to execute. (Configured in Space > Platform Components > Agents)

parameter.label: Handler Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the handler to execute. ex on-prem-incident-create

parameter.label: Payload
parameter.tooltip: The parameter-to-value mappings of the handler that is being executed in JSON format. ie {"baz": "buz"}

parameters.parameter:
<%= # Attachments -- OUT OF SCOPE FOR TEAMS RIGHT NOW
notification_template = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Template']['Submissions'])[0]
def replacesnippetsInRecord(record) replace_fields = ['Subject', 'HTML Content', 'Text Content'] snippet_re = /\$\{snippet\(\'(.*?)\'\)}/ #') #Putting a single quote and closing paren as a comment to make the above line not mess up the visual parsing of this code snippet_records = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Snippets']['Submissions'])
# Loop Over the Subject, HTML, Text of each Record and replace snippets if they were found record.each do |field, value| if replace_fields.include?(field) && !value.to_s.empty? value.scan(snippet_re).flatten.uniq.each do |snippetName| snippetRecord = snippetRecords.find { |snippet| snippet != nil && snippet["Name"] == snippetName } # If a snippet record is found, replace it if !snippetRecord.to_s.empty? # If it's an HTML field, use the HTML Content from the snippet if field.include? "HTML" record[field].gsub!("${snippet('#{snippetName}')}", snippetRecord["HTML Content"]) else # Otherwise, use the Text Content (Subject / Text) record[field].gsub!("${snippet('#{snippetName}')}", snippetRecord["Text Content"]) end end end end end return record end
def apply_replacements(content) replace_values = JSON.parse(@results['Replacement Values For Email']['output']) notification_date_formats = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Date Formats']['submissions']) date_format_json = notification_date_formats.each_with_object({}) do |item, hash| hash[item['name']] = { 'Format' => item['format'] } end
#Extract the keys from the replacement_values attribute replace_values.keys.each{|key| content = content.gsub(/\$\{#{key}\('(.*?)'\)\}/) do if replace_values[key].has_key?($1) replace_values[key][$1] else "${#{key}('#{$1}')}" end end }
#Replace Dates #This does a "negative look ahead" to ensure it captures the entire quote string content = content.gsub(/\$\{appearance\('(.*?)'\)\}(?!('\)\}))/) do #need to split into content and ${format()} sections thisContent=$1.split("$") #check if tag matches a valid date and if so convert first part into date and format it. thisDateFormat = thisContent[1].gsub(/\{format\('(.*?)'\)\}/) do if date_format_json.has_key?($1) date_format_json[$1]['Format'] else '${format(\''+$1+'\')}' end end if !thisDateFormat.nil? && !thisDateFormat.include?('{format') if thisDateFormat.include?('%') begin d = DateTime.parse(thisContent[0]) d.strftime(thisDateFormat) rescue thisContent[0].to_s end end else '${appearance(\''+thisContent[0]+'$'+thisDateFormat+'\')}' end end
return content end
template_to_use = nil # Do this twice since replacements can use snippets in very advanced message templates 2.times do template_to_use = replacesnippetsInRecord(notification_template) template_to_use["Subject"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["Subject"]) template_to_use["HTML Content"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["HTML Content"]) template_to_use["Text Content"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["Text Content"]) end
target_id = JSON.parse(@results['Get Slack Id by Email - Agent']['Response Body'])['user']['id']
{ "error_handling" => "Raise Error", "method" => "GET", "path" => "/chat.postMessage", "body" => { "channel" => target_id, "text" = "#{template_to_use['Subject']}\n\n#{template_to_use['Text Content']}" }.to_json }.to_json %>

parameter.label: Error Handling
parameter.tooltip: Determine what to return if an error is encountered.

parameter.label: Agent Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the agent that contains the handler to execute. (Configured in Space > Platform Components > Agents)

parameter.label: Handler Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the handler to execute. ex on-prem-incident-create

parameter.label: Payload
parameter.tooltip: The parameter-to-value mappings of the handler that is being executed in JSON format. ie {"baz": "buz"}

parameters.parameter:
<%= channels = JSON.parse(@results['Get Slack Channels - Agent']['Response Body']) target = JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['target']
# Find the channel with matching name and get its ID channel_id = channels['Channels'].find { |channel| channel['Name'] == target }&.dig('Id') %>

parameter.label: Error Handling
parameter.tooltip: Determine what to return if an error is encountered.

parameter.label: Agent Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the agent that contains the handler to execute. (Configured in Space > Platform Components > Agents)

parameter.label: Handler Slug
parameter.tooltip: The slug of the handler to execute. ex on-prem-incident-create

parameter.label: Payload
parameter.tooltip: The parameter-to-value mappings of the handler that is being executed in JSON format. ie {"baz": "buz"}

parameters.parameter:
<%= # Attachments -- OUT OF SCOPE FOR TEAMS RIGHT NOW
notification_template = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Template']['Submissions'])[0]
def replacesnippetsInRecord(record) replace_fields = ['Subject', 'HTML Content', 'Text Content'] snippet_re = /\$\{snippet\(\'(.*?)\'\)}/ #') #Putting a single quote and closing paren as a comment to make the above line not mess up the visual parsing of this code snippet_records = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Snippets']['Submissions'])
# Loop Over the Subject, HTML, Text of each Record and replace snippets if they were found record.each do |field, value| if replace_fields.include?(field) && !value.to_s.empty? value.scan(snippet_re).flatten.uniq.each do |snippetName| snippetRecord = snippetRecords.find { |snippet| snippet != nil && snippet["Name"] == snippetName } # If a snippet record is found, replace it if !snippetRecord.to_s.empty? # If it's an HTML field, use the HTML Content from the snippet if field.include? "HTML" record[field].gsub!("${snippet('#{snippetName}')}", snippetRecord["HTML Content"]) else # Otherwise, use the Text Content (Subject / Text) record[field].gsub!("${snippet('#{snippetName}')}", snippetRecord["Text Content"]) end end end end end return record end
def apply_replacements(content) replace_values = JSON.parse(@results['Replacement Values For Email']['output']) notification_date_formats = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Date Formats']['submissions']) date_format_json = notification_date_formats.each_with_object({}) do |item, hash| hash[item['name']] = { 'Format' => item['format'] } end
#Extract the keys from the replacement_values attribute replace_values.keys.each{|key| content = content.gsub(/\$\{#{key}\('(.*?)'\)\}/) do if replace_values[key].has_key?($1) replace_values[key][$1] else "${#{key}('#{$1}')}" end end }
#Replace Dates #This does a "negative look ahead" to ensure it captures the entire quote string content = content.gsub(/\$\{appearance\('(.*?)'\)\}(?!('\)\}))/) do #need to split into content and ${format()} sections thisContent=$1.split("$") #check if tag matches a valid date and if so convert first part into date and format it. thisDateFormat = thisContent[1].gsub(/\{format\('(.*?)'\)\}/) do if date_format_json.has_key?($1) date_format_json[$1]['Format'] else '${format(\''+$1+'\')}' end end if !thisDateFormat.nil? && !thisDateFormat.include?('{format') if thisDateFormat.include?('%') begin d = DateTime.parse(thisContent[0]) d.strftime(thisDateFormat) rescue thisContent[0].to_s end end else '${appearance(\''+thisContent[0]+'$'+thisDateFormat+'\')}' end end
return content end
template_to_use = nil # Do this twice since replacements can use snippets in very advanced message templates 2.times do template_to_use = replacesnippetsInRecord(notification_template) template_to_use["Subject"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["Subject"]) template_to_use["HTML Content"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["HTML Content"]) template_to_use["Text Content"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["Text Content"]) end
target_id = @results['Find Channel Id']['output']
{ "error_handling" => "Raise Error", "method" => "GET", "path" => "/chat.postMessage", "body" => { "channel" => target_id, "text" = "#{template_to_use['Subject']}\n\n#{template_to_use['Text Content']}" }.to_json }.to_json %>

<%= require 'net/http' require 'json' require 'uri'
# Attachments -- OUT OF SCOPE FOR TEAMS RIGHT NOW
notification_template = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Template']['Submissions'])[0]
def replacesnippetsInRecord(record) replace_fields = ['Subject', 'HTML Content', 'Text Content'] snippet_re = /\$\{snippet\(\'(.*?)\'\)}/ #') #Putting a single quote and closing paren as a comment to make the above line not mess up the visual parsing of this code snippet_records = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Snippets']['Submissions'])
# Loop Over the Subject, HTML, Text of each Record and replace snippets if they were found record.each do |field, value| if replace_fields.include?(field) && !value.to_s.empty? value.scan(snippet_re).flatten.uniq.each do |snippetName| snippetRecord = snippetRecords.find { |snippet| snippet != nil && snippet["Name"] == snippetName } # If a snippet record is found, replace it if !snippetRecord.to_s.empty? # If it's an HTML field, use the HTML Content from the snippet if field.include? "HTML" record[field].gsub!("${snippet('#{snippetName}')}", snippetRecord["HTML Content"]) else # Otherwise, use the Text Content (Subject / Text) record[field].gsub!("${snippet('#{snippetName}')}", snippetRecord["Text Content"]) end end end end end return record end
def apply_replacements(content) replace_values = JSON.parse(@results['Replacement Values For Email']['output']) notification_date_formats = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Date Formats']['submissions']) date_format_json = notification_date_formats.each_with_object({}) do |item, hash| hash[item['name']] = { 'Format' => item['format'] } end
#Extract the keys from the replacement_values attribute replace_values.keys.each{|key| content = content.gsub(/\$\{#{key}\('(.*?)'\)\}/) do if replace_values[key].has_key?($1) replace_values[key][$1] else "${#{key}('#{$1}')}" end end }
#Replace Dates #This does a "negative look ahead" to ensure it captures the entire quote string content = content.gsub(/\$\{appearance\('(.*?)'\)\}(?!('\)\}))/) do #need to split into content and ${format()} sections thisContent=$1.split("$") #check if tag matches a valid date and if so convert first part into date and format it. thisDateFormat = thisContent[1].gsub(/\{format\('(.*?)'\)\}/) do if date_format_json.has_key?($1) date_format_json[$1]['Format'] else '${format(\''+$1+'\')}' end end if !thisDateFormat.nil? && !thisDateFormat.include?('{format') if thisDateFormat.include?('%') begin d = DateTime.parse(thisContent[0]) d.strftime(thisDateFormat) rescue thisContent[0].to_s end end else '${appearance(\''+thisContent[0]+'$'+thisDateFormat+'\')}' end end
return content end
template_to_use = nil # Do this twice since replacements can use snippets in very advanced message templates 2.times do template_to_use = replacesnippetsInRecord(notification_template) template_to_use["Subject"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["Subject"]) template_to_use["HTML Content"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["HTML Content"]) template_to_use["Text Content"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["Text Content"]) end
webhook_url = JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Teams or Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['target'] uri = URI(webhook_url)
# Your message data message = { "attachments": [ { "contentType": "application/vnd.microsoft.card.adaptive", "content": { "type": "AdaptiveCard", "version": "1.4", "body": [ { "type": "TextBlock", "text": "#{template_to_use["Subject"]}\n\n#{template_to_use["Text Content"]}", "wrap": true } ] } } ] }
# Create the HTTP request http = Net::HTTP.new(uri.host, uri.port) http.use_ssl = true
request = Net::HTTP::Post.new(uri) request['Content-Type'] = 'application/json' request.body = message.to_json
# Send it response = http.request(request)
{ "code" => response.code, "body" => response.body }.to_json %>

<%= require 'net/http' require 'json' require 'uri'
# Attachments -- OUT OF SCOPE FOR TEAMS RIGHT NOW
notification_template = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Template']['Submissions'])[0]
def replacesnippetsInRecord(record) replace_fields = ['Subject', 'HTML Content', 'Text Content'] snippet_re = /\$\{snippet\(\'(.*?)\'\)}/ #') #Putting a single quote and closing paren as a comment to make the above line not mess up the visual parsing of this code snippet_records = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Snippets']['Submissions'])
# Loop Over the Subject, HTML, Text of each Record and replace snippets if they were found record.each do |field, value| if replace_fields.include?(field) && !value.to_s.empty? value.scan(snippet_re).flatten.uniq.each do |snippetName| snippetRecord = snippetRecords.find { |snippet| snippet != nil && snippet["Name"] == snippetName } # If a snippet record is found, replace it if !snippetRecord.to_s.empty? # If it's an HTML field, use the HTML Content from the snippet if field.include? "HTML" record[field].gsub!("${snippet('#{snippetName}')}", snippetRecord["HTML Content"]) else # Otherwise, use the Text Content (Subject / Text) record[field].gsub!("${snippet('#{snippetName}')}", snippetRecord["Text Content"]) end end end end end return record end
def apply_replacements(content) replace_values = JSON.parse(@results['Replacement Values For Email']['output']) notification_date_formats = JSON.parse(@results['Get Notification Date Formats']['submissions']) date_format_json = notification_date_formats.each_with_object({}) do |item, hash| hash[item['name']] = { 'Format' => item['format'] } end
#Extract the keys from the replacement_values attribute replace_values.keys.each{|key| content = content.gsub(/\$\{#{key}\('(.*?)'\)\}/) do if replace_values[key].has_key?($1) replace_values[key][$1] else "${#{key}('#{$1}')}" end end }
#Replace Dates #This does a "negative look ahead" to ensure it captures the entire quote string content = content.gsub(/\$\{appearance\('(.*?)'\)\}(?!('\)\}))/) do #need to split into content and ${format()} sections thisContent=$1.split("$") #check if tag matches a valid date and if so convert first part into date and format it. thisDateFormat = thisContent[1].gsub(/\{format\('(.*?)'\)\}/) do if date_format_json.has_key?($1) date_format_json[$1]['Format'] else '${format(\''+$1+'\')}' end end if !thisDateFormat.nil? && !thisDateFormat.include?('{format') if thisDateFormat.include?('%') begin d = DateTime.parse(thisContent[0]) d.strftime(thisDateFormat) rescue thisContent[0].to_s end end else '${appearance(\''+thisContent[0]+'$'+thisDateFormat+'\')}' end end
return content end
template_to_use = nil # Do this twice since replacements can use snippets in very advanced message templates 2.times do template_to_use = replacesnippetsInRecord(notification_template) template_to_use["Subject"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["Subject"]) template_to_use["HTML Content"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["HTML Content"]) template_to_use["Text Content"] = apply_replacements(template_to_use["Text Content"]) end
webhook_url = JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Teams or Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['target'] uri = URI(webhook_url)
# Your message data message = { "attachments": [ { "contentType": "application/vnd.microsoft.card.adaptive", "content": { "type": "AdaptiveCard", "version": "1.4", "body": [ { "type": "TextBlock", "text": "#{template_to_use["Subject"]}\n\n#{template_to_use["Text Content"]}", "wrap": true } ] } } ] }
# Create the HTTP request http = Net::HTTP.new(uri.host, uri.port) http.use_ssl = true
request = Net::HTTP::Post.new(uri) request['Content-Type'] = 'application/json' request.body = message.to_json
# Send it response = http.request(request)
{ "code" => response.code, "body" => response.body }.to_json %>

task.label: Broadcast
task.value: JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Teams or Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['type'].downcase == "broadcast"

task.label: Individual
task.value: JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Teams or Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['type'].downcase == "individual"

task.label: Individual
task.value: @results['Get Client Configuration']['_Returned Count'].to_i > 0 && !JSON.parse(@results['Get Client Configuration']['submissions'])[0]['agent handler slack'].to_s.empty? && JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['type'].downcase == "individual"

task.label: Broadcast
task.value: @results['Get Client Configuration']['_Returned Count'].to_i > 0 && !JSON.parse(@results['Get Client Configuration']['submissions'])[0]['agent handler slack'].to_s.empty? && JSON.parse(@results['Send Message - Slack - Loop Start']['Value'])['type'].downcase == "broadcast"
